pick.rationale: Miami holds a meaningful advantage in efficiency metrics, ranking 30-35th nationally compared to Missouri's 46-52nd range, with roughly a 5-point adjusted efficiency margin gap favoring the Hurricanes. The Hurricanes feature All-ACC performers Malik Reneau and Tre Donaldson who led Miami to a 25-8 record and ACC Tournament success, while Missouri's 20-12 record and bubble-team status reflect a less dominant season. Although 7/10 seed matchups are historically competitive and Miami's ACC semifinal blowout loss to Virginia raises some form concerns, the Hurricanes' superior overall profile, power conference pedigree, and significant efficiency advantage should prove decisive in this Round of 64 contest.

reasoningStep.title: Miami (FL) vs Missouri - West 7/10 Seed Analysis
reasoningStep.summary: Miami holds a clear efficiency and record advantage over Missouri in this 7/10 seed matchup, though the historical competitiveness of this seed pairing and Miami's recent ACC semifinal loss introduce some uncertainty.
reasoningStep.evidence: Miami ranks 30-35th in KenPom/Torvik vs Missouri's 46-52nd, with a 25-8 record compared to Missouri's 20-12. Miami's adjusted efficiency margin (~20.67) exceeds Missouri's (~15.36) by about 5 points. Key players Reneau and Donaldson earned All-ACC and NABC All-Region honors. Missouri got healthier with Jayden Stone's return from injury for the SEC Tournament, but Miami's overall quality and ACC pedigree provide the edge despite their 84-62 ACC semifinal loss to Virginia.

modelTrace.content:
You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.
